summary: App that allows the user to send/display text on another person's phone via a widget.
architectural_notes: App involves a sender side (text composer) and a receiver side (widget display); real-time or near-real-time message delivery is required.

existing_features.title: Create a Sender page where the user can enter a pairing code and compose a text message to send to the paired recipient device. The page should show confirmation when the message has been sent.

Create a Recipient/Widget page that displays the incoming text message for a given pairing code. The page should poll the backend at a regular interval (e.g., every 3 seconds) and update the displayed message in real time. The page should be designed to look like a full-screen widget suitable for display on a phone.

Create a Home/Landing page that allows the user to choose a role: 'Send a Message' (Sender) or 'Display Widget' (Recipient). From this page, generate or enter a pairing code to connect the two devices.

Apply a bold, high-contrast visual theme using dark backgrounds with vivid accent colors (e.g., electric green or neon orange), large typography, and a clean minimal layout. The widget display page should feel immersive and phone-screen-like. Enforce this theme consistently across all pages.
Promote the current draft version of the app to live mode so it is publicly accessible via a shareable URL.

On the Sender page, add a drawing canvas tab or toggle alongside the existing text composer, allowing the sender to sketch/draw freehand using mouse or touch input. The drawing should be composable as an image payload to send to the paired recipient device.

On the Recipient/Widget page, detect whether the incoming message payload is a drawing (image data URL) or plain text, and render it appropriately. If it is a drawing, display it as a full-screen image on the widget. If it is text, display it as before.

new_feature_requests.summary: Text input interface to compose a message to be sent to another device
Widget on the recipient's phone that displays the incoming text message
Mechanism to target/connect to a specific recipient's device (e.g., via device ID or pairing code)

Implement a backend actor that stores device/session pairing codes and associated messages. Support creating a new pairing code, registering a recipient session under a pairing code, sending a text message to a pairing code, and polling for the latest message by pairing code.
Allow the user to draw/sketch on the widget display, in addition to sending text

Extend the backend message storage to support larger payloads (base64-encoded image strings) without truncation. Ensure the existing sendMessage and pollMessage functions can handle drawing data URLs in addition to plain text strings.
